title: Running podman from a systemd unit file causes an explosion in CGroups
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

/kind bug

**Description**

Running Podman from a simple systemd unit file causes an explosion in CGroups in `/sys/fs/cgroup/systemd/libpod_parent/`

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create the following Systemd unit file as `/etc/systemd/system/podmantest.service`
```
[Unit]
Description=Podman Test Service
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
TimeoutStartSec=5m
ExecStartPre=-/usr/bin/podman rm "podmantest"

ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name podmantest fedora doesnotexist 120

ExecReload=-/usr/bin/podman stop "podmantest"
ExecReload=-/usr/bin/podman rm "podmantest"
ExecStop=-/usr/bin/podman stop "podmantest"
Restart=always
RestartSec=30

[Install]
```

2. `systemctl daemon-reload` to pick up the unit

3. `systemctl start podmantest.service`

**Describe the results you received:**

A bunch of CGroups created in `/sys/fs/cgroup/systemd/libpod_parent/` - I have 37 on my system

**Describe the results you expected:**
Either just one CGroup for the container and conmon, or none at all since we're using CGroupFS

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I feel like this is the construction of the unit file, but I'm insufficiently familar with systemd to build one that doesn't cause this.
